title: Command 'start' not found due to permission issues
symptom: After updating n8n in a Docker environment, the error 'Command start not found' appears in the logs, preventing access to the interface.
not_this_if: Error occurs without updating or in a different environment
root_cause: The error occurs because the necessary permissions for the subfolder where n8n is installed were not set correctly. Without proper permissions, n8n cannot execute commands as expected.
fix_steps: Access the terminal of the Docker container
Navigate to the n8n installation directory
Run the command 'chmod -R 755 <subfolder>' to set the correct permissions recursively
agent_instruction: Ensure the subfolder permissions are set correctly using the chmod command.
